questions.title: Student Performance Analysis System
questions.question: Design a Python-based system for analyzing student performance in a class.
requirements: Store student data using appropriate data types (list, dictionary)
Each student should have: Name (string), Marks of 4 subjects (list of integers)
Create a class named StudentAnalysis that handles all operations
The class should include the following methods:
- A method to add student data (name and marks)
- A method to calculate average marks using a loop
- A method to find maximum and minimum marks using loops
- A method to find the top-performing student based on average marks
- A method to display a full report of all students
Use loops for calculations
Use functions inside a class
Use proper data structures (list + dictionary)

questions.title: Library Management System
questions.question: Create a Python-based library management system using Object-Oriented Programming.
requirements: Design a Book class with properties: title, author, isbn, available_copies
Design a Library class to manage multiple books
Implement methods to: add books, remove books, issue books, return books, search books
Track book availability and maintain a record of issued books
Display library inventory with available and issued copies
Use proper error handling for invalid operations
Use lists and dictionaries to store book and member data

questions.title: Stack and Queue Implementation
questions.question: Implement Stack and Queue data structures from scratch.
requirements: Create a Stack class with push(), pop(), peek(), isEmpty() methods
Create a Queue class with enqueue(), dequeue(), peek(), isEmpty() methods
Use lists or linked list concept for internal storage
Implement size tracking for both structures
Add input validation and error handling
Write test cases to demonstrate both structures working correctly
Include documentation for each method
